mnemonics: CAKE: To check if a function is an inner product, verify Conjugate symmetry, Additivity, positive definiteness (Kernel), and homogeneity.
keyPoints: An inner product is a generalization of the dot product that satisfies conjugate symmetry, linearity in the first argument, and positive-definiteness.
The norm (length) of a vector is derived from the inner product as ||v|| = √⟨v,v⟩.
The Cauchy-Schwarz inequality |⟨u,v⟩| ≤ ||u|| ||v|| is fundamental in inner product spaces.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to take the complex conjugate when working in complex vector spaces (conjugate symmetry).
Assuming linearity works the same in both arguments (it's conjugate linear in the second argument for complex spaces).
Confusing the properties of inner products with those of norms or metrics.
